blocks:
Sūrat Aṣ-Ṣāffāt (The Rangers)

Meccan. Its number of verses is one hundred and eighty-one verses according to the Basran count, and two verses more in the remaining counts. Its points of difference are two verses. *وَما كانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ ** is counted [as a separate verse] by others besides the Basran counter. And all of them count *وَإِنْ كانُوا لَيَقُولُونَ ** except for Abū Ja‘far. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالصَّافَّاتِ صَفًّا ** (By those who range themselves in ranks) Al-Qummi said: [This refers to] the angels, the prophets, and whoever ranges himself in ranks for Allah and worships Him. (٢) فَالزَّاجِراتِ زَجْراً ** (and those who drive away with a driving) He said: [This refers to] those who reprimand the people. (٣) فَالتَّالِياتِ ذِكْراً ** (and those who recite the remembrance) He said: [This refers to] those among the people who recite the Book (الكتاب). He said: So, this is an oath (قسم) and its answer (جوابه). (٤) إِنَّ إِلهَكُمْ لَواحِدٌ. (Your God is indeed one) (٥) رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما وَرَبُّ الْمَشارِقِ ** (Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and Lord of the easts.) [This refers to] the easts of the stars (مشارق الكواكب) or the easts of the sun (مشارق الشمس), for it has an east every day. And corresponding to them are the wests (المغارب). Therefore, mentioning them was sufficient, even though the rising (الشروق) is more indicative of power (القدرة) and more eloquent regarding the blessing (النعمة).

(٦) إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا السَّماءَ الدُّنْيا ** (The lowest heaven) means the one nearest (القربى) to you بِزِينَةٍ الْكَواكِبِ ** And it was read (وقرئ) with tanwin on (زينة) and the jarr (genitive case) on (الكواكب) and the nasb (accusative case) on it. (6) Indeed We have adorned the lowest heaven with the adornment of the stars (٧) وَحِفْظاً مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطانٍ مارِدٍ ** (And as a safeguard) by the throwing of the meteors (الشّهب). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: al-Marid (المارد) is the wicked (الخبيث). (7) and as a safeguard against every rebellious devil. (٨) لا يَسَّمَّعُونَ إِلَى الْمَلَإِ الْأَعْلى ** al-Mala’ al-A’la (الْمَلَإِ الْأَعْلى) means the angels (الملائكة) and their nobles (واشرافهم). And it was read (وقرئ) with tashdid (بالتشديد) from al-tassamu' (التسمّع), which is seeking to hear (تطلّب السماع). وَيُقْذَفُونَ ** And they are pelted (ويرمون). (8) They cannot listen to the Supreme Assembly, and are pelted Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: (It means) the stars (الكواكب) with which they are pelted مِنْ كُلِّ جانِبٍ ** (from every direction) of the sides (جوانب) of the heaven when they intend to ascend it.

(٩) دُحُوراً ** (9) [It is] for the repelling (الدُّحُور), which is expulsion. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ واصِبٌ. (9) and there is a perpetual punishment for them. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, perpetual (دائم), painful (موجع), which has reached their hearts. (١٠) إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الْخَطْفَةَ ** (10) except him who snatches a snatch, [It means] he snatched the speech of the angels secretly (مسارقة). فَأَتْبَعَهُ Then He pursues him, meaning, it follows him. شِهابٌ ثاقِبٌ ** whereat a piercing flame pursues him. Luminous, as if it pierces (يثقب) the atmosphere (الجوّ) with its light. And the Shihab (الشهاب) is what is seen as if a star has fallen. Al-Qummi [said]: And it is that with which they are pelted, so they are burned. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the Hadith of the Ascension (المعراج), he said: Then Gabriel ascended, and I ascended with him to the lowest heaven, and upon it was an angel called Isma'il, and he is the master (صاحب) of the snatching (الخطفة) about which Allah said: إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُ شِهابٌ ثاقِبٌ ** (except him who snatches a snatch, whereat a piercing flame pursues him). And beneath him are seventy thousand angels, and beneath every angel are seventy thousand angels. The Hadith [continues], and it has already passed.

**(١١) فَاسْتَفْتِهِمْ ** فاستخبرهم ** أَهُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقاً أَمْ مَنْ خَلَقْنا ** (11) So ask them: Are they harder to create, or those We have created? (1) meaning, than the angels, the heavens, the earth, what is between them, the risings, the stars, and the piercing meteors. ** إِنَّا خَلَقْناهُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ لازِبٍ. ** (12) Indeed, We created them from an adhesive clay. Al-Qummi said: meaning, it sticks to the hand.

(١٢) بَلْ عَجِبْتَ ** (Nay, you are surprised) [is] at the power of Allah and their denial of the Resurrection. And it was recited with the dammah on the tā’ (بضمّ التاء). And [the author] in al-Jawami' attributed it to Ali (peace be upon him). وَيَسْخَرُونَ ** (and they scoff) [They scoff] at your surprise (تعجّبك) or at whoever describes Me with power (القدرة). (١٣) وَإِذا ذُكِّرُوا لا يَذْكُرُونَ ** (and when they are reminded, they do not take the reminder.) [It means] when they are admonished with something, they are not admonished by it, or when that which indicates the soundness of the Gathering (الحشر) is mentioned to them, they do not benefit from it due to their dullness (لبلادتهم) and lack of thought (وقلّة فكرهم). (١٤) وَإِذا رَأَوْا آيَةً ** (And when they see a sign) [meaning] a miracle (معجزة) that indicates the truthfulness of the one who states it, يَسْتَسْخِرُونَ ** (they seek to scoff.) [Meaning] they exaggerate in mockery (السخرية) and say that it is magic, or some of them invite others to mock it. (١٥) وَقالُوا إِنْ هذا ** (and say, 'This is nothing) They mean what they see, ** إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (but manifest magic.) [meaning] its magical nature (سحريّته) is apparent. (١٦) أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً وَعِظاماً أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ ** (When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we indeed be resurrected?) They exaggerated in denial, especially in this state. And it was recited by omitting the first hamzah (الهمزة) sometimes, and the second hamzah (الهمزة) at other times.

footnote (١) And it is said [it refers] to the past nations (*الأمم الماضية*) and the bygone generations (*القرون السالفة*). He means that they are not wiser in creation than others among the nations whom We destroyed.

** أَوَآباؤُنَا الْأَوَّلُونَ (١٧) ** (17) Or our forefathers of yore? And it was recited with the sukūn (سكون) on the wāw (واو) in أوَ.

** قُلْ نَعَمْ وَأَنْتُمْ داخِرُونَ (١٨) ** (18) Say, 'Yes, and you will be abased.' Abased (صاغرون).

** فَإِنَّما هِيَ زَجْرَةٌ واحِدَةٌ (١٩) ** (19) It will be just a single shout, For the resurrection (البعثة) is only a single shout (صيحة واحدة), which is the second blast (النفخة الثانية), like the herder's rebuke (زجر) of his flock when he shouts at them. ** فَإِذا هُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ ** and behold, they will be looking on. Then behold, they will be standing up from their resting places (مراقد), alive, seeing, or waiting for what will be done with them.

(٢٠) ** وَقالُوا يا وَيْلَنا هذا يَوْمُ الدِّينِ ** (20) and they will say, 'Woe to us! This is the Day of Retribution!' The Day of Reckoning and Recompense (يوم الحساب والمجازاة).

(٢١) ** هذا يَوْمُ الْفَصْلِ الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ ** (21) 'This is the Day of Judgement which you used to deny.' The response of the angels, or the saying of some of them to others. And الْفَصْلِ (al-Fasl) is the judgment (القضاء) and the distinction (الفرق) between the good-doer (المحسن) and the evil-doer (المسيء).

(٢٢) ** احْشُرُوا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا. (22) 'Gather those who did wrong, Al-Qummi said: الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (those who did wrong) refers to the family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) regarding their right (حقّهم). وَأَزْواجَهُمْ ** (and their spouses) means their likes (وأشباههم). وَما كانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ.**

(٢٣) ** مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ فَاهْدُوهُمْ إِلى صِراطِ الْجَحِيمِ. (23) and their mates, and what they used to worship instead of Allah, and guide them to the path of hell. Referring to idols (الأصنام) and other things, as an increase in their regret (تحسيرهم) and shame (تخجيلهم). فَاهْدُوهُمْ إِلى صِراطِ الْجَحِيمِ. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: Call them (ادعوهم) to the path of Hell (طريق الجحيم).

(٢٤) ** وَقِفُوهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ مَسْؤُلُونَ ** (24) And stop them! They must be questioned.' ** وَقِفُوهُمْ ** (And stop them!) means detain them (احبسوهم) in the station (الموقف). إِنَّهُمْ مَسْؤُلُونَ ** (They must be questioned) It was said: about their beliefs (عقائدهم) and their deeds (أعمالهم). And Al-Qummi said: about the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And similar to this is found in al-Amali and al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family). And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), that he said in the interpretation of this verse: "The feet of a servant shall not move until he is questioned about four things: about his youth, in what he wore it out; about his life, in what he spent it; about his wealth, from where he acquired it and in what he spent it; and about our love, the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them)."

(٢٥) ما لَكُمْ لا تَناصَرُونَ ** (25) What is the matter with you that you do not help one another? None of you helps the other by deliverance (بالتخليص), and this is a rebuke (توبيخ) and a reproach (تقريع). (٢٦) بَلْ هُمُ الْيَوْمَ مُسْتَسْلِمُونَ ** (26) Rather, today they are submissive. They are compliant (منقادون) due to their incapacity, or they are mutually surrendering (متسالمون), where some surrender others and abandon them. Al-Qummi said: It means the punishment (العذاب). (٢٧) وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ يَتَساءَلُونَ ** (27) And some of them will turn to others, questioning. Some question others for the purpose of rebuke (للتوبيخ). (٢٨) قالُوا إِنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ تَأْتُونَنا عَنِ الْيَمِينِ ** (28) They will say, 'Indeed, you used to approach us from the right side.' It was said that it means from the strongest (أقوى) and most favorable (أيمنه) of aspects. (٢٩) قالُوا بَلْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ. (29) They will say, 'Rather, you yourselves were not faithful.' (٣٠) وَما كانَ لَنا عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطانٍ بَلْ كُنْتُمْ قَوْماً طاغِينَ (30) 'We had no authority over you; rather, you were a rebellious lot.' (٣١) فَحَقَّ عَلَيْنا قَوْلُ رَبِّنا إِنَّا لَذائِقُونَ. (31) 'So the word of our Lord has become due against us: We shall indeed taste [the punishment].' Al-Qummi said: The punishment (العذاب). (٣٢) فَأَغْوَيْناكُمْ إِنَّا كُنَّا غاوِينَ. (32) 'So we led you astray, for we ourselves were astray.' (٣٣) فَإِنَّهُمْ For the followers (الأتباع) and the followed (المتبوعين) يَوْمَئِذٍ فِي الْعَذابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ ** (33) Indeed, on that day they will share in the punishment, just as they shared in misguidance (الغواية). (٣٤) إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَفْعَلُ بِالْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (34) Indeed, that is how We deal with the guilty. with the polytheists (بالمشركين). (٣٥) إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا إِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا اللهُ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ. (35) Indeed, when it was said to them, 'There is no god except Allah,' they used to be arrogant. (٣٦) وَيَقُولُونَ أَإِنَّا لَتارِكُوا آلِهَتِنا لِشاعِرٍ مَجْنُونٍ ** (36) And they would say, 'Shall we abandon our gods for a crazy poet?' They mean the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. (٣٧) بَلْ جاءَ بِالْحَقِّ وَصَدَّقَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (37) Rather, he has brought the truth and confirmed the apostles. This is a rebuttal against them, [stating] that what He brought concerning Monotheism (التوحيد) is the truth, for which the proof (البرهان) has been established and upon which the Messengers (المرسلون) have agreed. (٣٨) إِنَّكُمْ لَذائِقُوا الْعَذابِ الْأَلِيمِ ** (38) You will indeed taste the painful punishment, due to polytheism (بالإشراك) and the denial of the Messenger (الرسول). (٣٩) وَما تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. (39) and you will not be requited except for what you used to do. (٤٠) إِلَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (40) except the sincere servants of Allah. This is an interrupted exception (استثناء منقطع). (٤١) أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ رِزْقٌ مَعْلُومٌ. (41) For them there is a known provision: (٤٢) فَواكِهُ وَهُمْ مُكْرَمُونَ. (42) fruits, and they will be honoured. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a hadith describing the people of Paradise, He said: "As for His saying: أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ رِزْقٌ مَعْلُومٌ ** (41) For them there is a known provision, He said: 'The servants (الخدّام) know it, so they bring it to the friends of Allah (أولياء الله) before they ask them for it.' And as for His saying: فَواكِهُ وَهُمْ مُكْرَمُونَ ** (42) fruits, and they will be honoured, He said: 'They will not desire anything in Paradise except that they will be honored with it.'"

(٤٣) فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ. (43) In gardens of bliss. (٤٤) عَلى سُرُرٍ مُتَقابِلِينَ. (44) on couches, facing one another. (٤٥) يُطافُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِكَأْسٍ ** (45) They will be served around with a cup (with a vessel containing wine) مِنْ مَعِينٍ ** (from a flowing drink) or a river مَعِينٍ ** meaning, flowing, visible to the eyes (للعيون), or issuing forth from springs (العيون). The wine of Paradise (خمر الجنّة) is described by this because it flows like water. (٤٦) بَيْضاءَ لَذَّةٍ لِلشَّارِبِينَ ** (46) white, delicious to the drinkers. It is said that its description as "delicious (لَذَّةٍ)" is either for emphasis (للمبالغة) or because it is the feminine form of لَذّ (ladhdh) meaning delicious (لذيذ). (٤٧) لا فِيها غَوْلٌ ** (47) There is no taint in it, (no harm (غائلة) or corruption, as is found in the wine of this world (خمر الدنيا), such as hangover (كالخمار)) وَلا هُمْ عَنْها يُنْزَفُونَ ** (nor will they be intoxicated by it). It is said this means they will not become drunk (يسكرون), derived from نزف (nazafa) when one's intellect departs. And al-Qummi said: meaning, they will not be driven away (لا يطردون) from it. And it was read with a kasrah on the zay (يُنْزِفُونَ). (٤٨) وَعِنْدَهُمْ قاصِراتُ الطَّرْفِ ** (48) And with them will be maidens restraining their glances, (who have restricted their gazes (أبصارهنّ) to their husbands) عِينٌ ** (‘īn), meaning wide-eyed (عيناء). It is interpreted sometimes as those with wide, beautiful eyes (واسعات العيون لحسانها), and at other times as those with intensely white eyes and intensely black pupils (الشديدة بياض العين الشّديدة سوادها). (٤٩) كَأَنَّهُنَّ بَيْضٌ مَكْنُونٌ ** (49) as if they were protected eggs. He likened them to the eggs of the ostrich (بيض النعام) which it protects (تكنّه) with its feathers, preserved from dust and the like, due to their purity (الصفا) and whiteness mixed with the slightest yellowness (أدنى صفرة), for that is the most beautiful of body colors, as has been said. (٥٠) فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ يَتَساءَلُونَ ** (50) Then some of them will turn to others, questioning one another. (about knowledge (المعارف), virtues (الفضائل), and what transpired for them and against them in the world (الدنيا), for that is the most delightful of delights, as it was said: "And nothing remains of the delights except the narratives of the noble ones (أحاديث الكرام) over the wine (على المدام).") (٥١) قالَ قائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ ** (51) A speaker among them will say, (in their conversation) إِنِّي كانَ لِي قَرِينٌ ** ('Indeed, I had a companion) (a close companion (جليس) in the world (الدنيا).) (٥٢) يَقُولُ أَإِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُصَدِّقِينَ ** (52) who used to say, "Are you truly one of those who confirm [the resurrection]? (He would reproach me (يوبّخني) for confirming the Resurrection (التصديق بالبعث).) (٥٣) أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً وَعِظاماً أَإِنَّا لَمَدِينُونَ ** (53) When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we indeed be requited?"' (meaning, shall we indeed be requited (لمجزيّون), derived from الدّين (al-dīn) in the sense of recompense (الجزاء).) (٥٤) قالَ ** (54) He will say, (meaning, that speaker said to his companions) هَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُطَّلِعُونَ ** (Do you wish to look down) toward the inhabitants of the Fire (أهل النار) so that I may show you that companion (القرين)? And it is said that the speaker is Allah or one of the angels, saying to them: Do you wish to look down upon the inhabitants of the Fire (أهل النار) so that I may show you that companion (القرين), and thus you may know where your station is relative to their station?

(٥٥) فَاطَّلَعَ عليهم Then he looked down upon them (the inhabitants of the Fire). ** فَرَآهُ فِي سَوَاءِ الْجَحِيمِ ** (55) Then he looked down and saw him in the middle of hell. and saw him—meaning his companion—in the middle of hell. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: in the middle (وَسَط) of Hell (الْجَحِيم). (٥٦) قَالَ تَاللَّهِ إِنْ كِدْتَ لَتُرْدِينِ ** (56) He said, 'By Allah, you had almost ruined me! He said, 'By Allah, you had almost ruined me! You had almost destroyed me through temptation (الإغواء). (٥٧) وَلَوْلَا نِعْمَةُ رَبِّي بالهداية والعصمة ** لَكُنْتُ مِنَ الْمُحْضَرِينَ ** (57) And had it not been for the blessing of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth [to the punishment] with you.' And had it not been for the blessing of my Lord—by guidance (الهداية) and protection (العصمة)—I would certainly have been among those brought forth with you in it. (٥٨) أَفَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَيِّتِينَ ** (58) 'Are we not going to die, This is conjoined to an omitted phrase, meaning: we are eternal, enjoying bliss. So we are not going to die—meaning: those whose nature is death. (٥٩) إِلَّا مَوْتَتَنَا الْأُولَى التي كانت في الدنيا ** وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ ** (59) except our first death, and shall we not be punished?' except our first death—which occurred in the world (الدنيا)—and shall we not be punished like the disbelievers (الْكُفَّار). (٦٠) إِنَّ هَذَا لَهُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ ** (60) This is indeed the great success. (٦١) لِمِثْلِ هَذَا فَلْيَعْمَلِ الْعَامِلُونَ ** (61) For the like of this let the workers work. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said: When the inhabitants of Paradise enter Paradise and the inhabitants of the Fire enter the Fire, Death (الْمَوْت) will be brought and slaughtered like a ram (الْكَبْش) between Paradise (الْجَنَّة) and the Fire (النَّار). Then it will be said: Eternity (خُلُود), so there is no death ever. Then the inhabitants of Paradise will say: Are we not going to die? (referencing the verses).

** أَذلِكَ خَيْرٌ نُزُلاً أَمْ شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّومِ ** (Is that a better hospitality, or the tree of Zaqqum?) A tree whose fruit is the provision (نزل) of the people of the Fire. And in it is an indication that what was mentioned of the bliss for the people of Paradise is equivalent to the status of what is prepared for the sojourner (النّازل), and they have what is beyond that, which the understandings (الافهام) fall short of comprehending. And likewise is al-Zaqqum for the people of the Fire. It is said that it is the name of a small-leafed, foul-smelling (ذفرة), bitter tree found in Tihamah (بتهامة), by which the described tree was named.

(٦٣) إِنَّا جَعَلْناها فِتْنَةً لِلظَّالِمِينَ ** (63) We have made it a trial for the wrongdoers. A trial (محنة) and a torment (عذاباً) for them in the Hereafter, or a test (ابتلاء) in this world.

In al-Majma': It is narrated that when Quraysh heard this Ayah: إِنَّ شَجَرَةَ الزَّقُّومِ طَعامُ الْأَثِيمِ ** (44) Indeed, the tree of Zaqqūm is the food of the vicious sinner. they said, "We do not know this tree." Ibn al-Ziba'ri said: الزَّقُّومِ (Zaqqūm), in the language of the Berbers (البربر), means dates and butter (التمر والزبد). And in one narration: [it means dates and butter] in the language of Yemen (اليمن). Then Abu Jahl said to his slave-girl (جاريته): "O slave-girl, give us زقّمينا (Zaqqim us)." The slave-girl brought him dates and butter. He then said to his companions: "Eat تزقّموا (Zaqqūm) of this, which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم frightens you with, claiming that the Fire grows trees, while the Fire burns trees." So Allah, the Glorified, revealed: إِنَّا جَعَلْناها فِتْنَةً لِلظَّالِمِينَ.** (63) We have made it a trial for the wrongdoers.

(٦٤) إِنَّها شَجَرَةٌ تَخْرُجُ فِي أَصْلِ الْجَحِيمِ ** (64) It is a tree that issues from the bottom of hell. Its place of growth (مَنبَتُها) is in the bottom (قَعْر) of Hell (جَهَنَّم), and its branches rise up to its levels (دَرَكَات). (٦٥) طَلْعُها ** (65) Its spathe Its fruit (حَمْلُها) is borrowed from the spathe (طَلْع) of the date palm. ** كَأَنَّهُ رُؤُسُ الشَّياطِينِ ** is as if it were the heads of satans. in the extremity of ugliness and terror. It was said that this is a simile using the imagined (بِالمُتَخَيَّل), like likening that which is supremely beautiful to an angel (بِالمَلَك). (٦٦) فَإِنَّهُمْ لَآكِلُونَ مِنْها فَمالِؤُنَ مِنْهَا الْبُطُونَ ** (66) They will indeed eat of it, and fill their bellies with it. due to the overwhelming hunger. (٦٧) ثُمَّ إِنَّ لَهُمْ عَلَيْها ** (67) Then they will have a mixture of boiling water over it. meaning, after they have become satiated with it, and thirst has overcome them, and their seeking of drink (اِسْتِقَاؤُهُم) has prolonged: ** لَشَوْباً مِنْ حَمِيمٍ ** (a mixture of boiling water) meaning, a drink (شَرَابًا) of ghassāq (غَسَّاق) or pus (صَدِيد) mixed (مَشُوبًا) with boiling water (مَاء حَمِيم) that cuts their intestines (أَمْعَاءَهُم). (٦٨) ثُمَّ إِنَّ مَرْجِعَهُمْ لَإِلَى الْجَحِيمِ ** (68) Then their return will indeed be to hell. For the Zaqqūm (الزّقّوم) and the boiling water (الحَمِيم) are provisions (نُزُل) presented to them before their entry into it. And it was said that the boiling water (الحَمِيم) is outside of it, due to the Almighty's saying: ** هذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ الَّتِي يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا الْمُجْرِمُونَ يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَها وَبَيْنَ حَمِيمٍ آنٍ ** (This is the hell that the guilty deny. They will circulate between it and boiling water that is intensely hot.) They are brought to it (يُورَدُونَ إِلَيْهِ) just as camels are brought to water, then they are returned (يُرَدُّونَ) to the Hellfire (الجَحِيم).

(٦٩) إِنَّهُمْ أَلْفَوْا آباءَهُمْ ضالِّينَ. (69) They found their fathers astray. (٧٠) فَهُمْ عَلى آثارِهِمْ يُهْرَعُونَ ** This is a justification for their deserving those severe punishments due to their imitation of their fathers in error (الضلال). And al-ihra' (الاهراع) is intense speed, as if they are being urged to hasten in their footsteps. And in it is an indication that they rushed to that without pausing for research (بحث) or consideration (نظر).

(٧١) وَلَقَدْ ضَلَّ قَبْلَهُمْ before your people أَكْثَرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ. (71) And certainly, before them, most of the former generations went astray. (٧٢) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا فِيهِمْ مُنْذِرِينَ prophets who warned them of the consequences. (72) And certainly We sent among them warners, (٧٣) فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ in terms of severity and horror. (73) so observe how was the fate of those who were warned, (٧٤) إِلَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ Except those who became aware through their warning, and thus made their religion sincere for Allah. And it was read with fathah (الْمُخْلَصِينَ), meaning: those whom Allah made sincere for His religion. The address is directed to the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the intent is to address his people, for they, too, heard their stories and saw their traces (آثارهم). (74) except the sincere servants of Allah.

(٧٥) وَلَقَدْ نادانا نُوحٌ ** (75) Certainly Noah called Us This is the beginning of detailing the stories after their summarization. Meaning: And certainly, he called upon Us when he despaired of his people. فَلَنِعْمَ الْمُجِيبُونَ ** —and how excellent are the responders! Meaning: So We responded to him with the best response. By Allah, We are indeed the excellent responders. (٧٦) وَنَجَّيْناهُ وَأَهْلَهُ مِنَ الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (76) We delivered him and his family from the great distress, From the harm (أذى) of his people and the drowning. (٧٧) وَجَعَلْنا ذُرِّيَّتَهُ هُمُ الْباقِينَ ** (77) and made his progeny the survivors, Since those who perished, perished. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He says: The truth (الحقّ), the prophethood (النبوّة), the Book (الكتاب), and the faith (الإيمان) are in his descendants (عقبه). And not everyone on earth among the Children of Adam is from the progeny of Noah. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said in His Book: ** احْمِلْ فِيها مِنْ كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَنْ سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ الْقَوْلُ وَمَنْ آمَنَ وَما آمَنَ مَعَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ ** (Carry in it a pair of every kind, and your family—excepting him against whom the decree has already gone forth—and those who have faith. And none had faith with him except a few.) And He also said: ** ذُرِّيَّةَ مَنْ حَمَلْنا مَعَ نُوحٍ ** (O descendants of those whom We carried with Noah!)

(٧٨) وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرِينَ ** (78) And We left for him [a good name] among the later generations. [Meaning] among the nations. (٧٩) سَلامٌ عَلى نُوحٍ فِي الْعالَمِينَ (79) Peace be to Noah among the nations! It was said: meaning, ** وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ ** (We left for him) among them the greeting with this word [سَلامٌ] and the supplication for its establishment among the angels and the two weighty ones (الثقلين). And it was said: rather, it is ** سَلامٌ ** (peace) from Allah upon him, and the object (مفعول) of تَرَكْنا (We left) is omitted, such as praise (الثنا). And in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a tradition: Noah gave them glad tidings of Hud and commanded them to follow him, and that they should establish the testament (الوصيّة) every year, look into it, and that it should be a festival (عيداً) for them, just as Adam had commanded them. Then the oppressors (الجبريّة) appeared from the descendants of Ham and Japheth, so the descendants of Sam concealed the knowledge they possessed. After Noah, the dominion (الدّولة) passed over Sam to Ham and Japheth. And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ** وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرِينَ ** (And We left for him [a good name] among the later generations). He says: I left upon Noah the dominion (دولة) of the tyrants (الجبّارين). And Allah consoles Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with that. He (the Imam) said: And the Sind, India, and the Abyssinians (الحبش) were born to Ham, and the Arabs and the non-Arabs (العجم) were born to Sam. And the dominion (الدولة) passed over them, and they inherited the testament (الوصيّة), generation after generation, until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Hud.

** إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (80) We thus reward the good-doers. meaning that it is a recompense for him due to his goodness (إحسانه). ** إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (81) Indeed, he was one of Our faithful servants. ** ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا الْآخَرِينَ ** (82) Then We drowned the others. meaning the disbelievers (كفّار) among his people. ** وَإِنَّ مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ ** (83) Indeed, Abraham was among his followers. meaning those who followed him (شايعه) in faith (الإيمان) and the fundamentals of the Sacred Law (أصول الشريعة), [is] indeed Abraham. In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "May the name be pleasing to you." It was said: "And what is it?" He said: "The الشيعة." It was said: "Indeed, people reproach us with that." He said: "Do you not hear the saying of Allah: وَإِنَّ مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ لَإِبْراهِيمَ ** (Indeed, Abraham was among his followers) and His saying: فَاسْتَغاثَهُ الَّذِي مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ عَلَى الَّذِي مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ. (So the one who was of his faction sought his help against the one who was of his enemy)?"

** إِذْ جاءَ رَبَّهُ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ ** (84) when he came to his Lord with a sound heart. [Meaning] free from the love of the world. Narrations concerning its meaning have already passed in Surat al-Shu'ara' (The Poets).

** إِذْ قالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ ما ذا تَعْبُدُونَ.** (85) when he said to his father and his people, 'What do you worship?'

** أَإِفْكاً آلِهَةً دُونَ اللهِ تُرِيدُونَ آلِهَةً دُونَ اللهِ إِفْكاً ** (86) 'Do you desire gods besides Allah, falsely?' [The word] ** إِفْكاً ** (falsely) was brought forward for emphasis (لِلْعِنَايَة).

** فَما ظَنُّكُمْ بِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (87) 'So what is your opinion of the Lord of all the worlds?' [Meaning:] concerning the One Who is truly deserving of worship (حَقِيق بِالْعِبَادَة), such that you associate others with Him and feel safe from His punishment.

** فَنَظَرَ نَظْرَةً فِي النُّجُومِ ** (88) Then he cast a glance at the stars, So he saw their positions (مَوَاقِعَهَا) and their conjunctions (اِتِّصَالَاتِهَا).

** فَقالَ إِنِّي سَقِيمٌ ** (89) and said, 'I am sick.' It was said: He showed them that he had deduced from them [the stars] that he was approaching sickness (مُشَارِف لِلسُّقْم), so that they would not take him out to their place of worship, because they were astrologers (مُنَجِّمِين). That was when they asked him to celebrate the festival (يُعَيِّد) with them. The majority of their illnesses was the plague (الطَّاعُون), and they feared contagion (الْعَدْوَى).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "By Allah, he was not sick, nor did he lie." And in al-Ma'ani and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), is a similar narration, with the addition: "He only meant sick in his religion (فِي دِينِهِ), seeking [the truth] (مُرْتَادًا)."

He said in al-Ma'ani: It has been narrated that he meant by His saying: ** إِنِّي سَقِيمٌ ** (I am sick), meaning: "I shall become sick (سَأَسْقَم)." And every dead person is sick (سَقِيم). And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to His Prophet: ** إِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ ** (You are mortal), meaning: "You shall die (سَتَمُوت)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah, he said: "He calculated (حَسَب) and saw what would befall al-Husayn (peace be upon him). ** فَقالَ إِنِّي سَقِيمٌ ** (and said, 'I am sick') due to what would befall al-Husayn."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created the Holy Spirit (رُوح الْقُدُس). He did not create a creature closer to Him than it, yet it is not the noblest of His creation to Him. When He wills a matter, He casts it upon it [the Holy Spirit], and it [the Holy Spirit] casts it upon the stars, and it [the matter] proceeds by it."

(٩٠) فَتَوَلَّوْا عَنْهُ مُدْبِرِينَ ** (٩٠) So they turned their backs on him, retreating. [They went] to a festival (عيد) of theirs. (٩١) فَراغَ إِلى آلِهَتِهِمْ ** (٩١) Then he turned secretly to their gods, and said, He went to them secretly. فَقالَ ** (and said), meaning to the idols, mockingly: أَلا تَأْكُلُونَ ** (Do you not eat?), meaning the food (الطّعام) that was with them. (٩٢) ما لَكُمْ لا تَنْطِقُونَ ** (٩٢) ‘What is the matter with you that you do not speak?’ with my reply (بجوابي) (1). (٩٣) فَراغَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (٩٣) Then he fell upon them, striking them with the right hand. He inclined towards them secretly, and the transitive usage with على (عَلَيْهِمْ) is for superiority (للاستعلاء) and the dislike of inclining [towards them]. ضَرْباً بِالْيَمِينِ (striking them with the right hand). He struck them a striking with it. (٩٤) فَأَقْبَلُوا إِلَيْهِ ** (٩٤) Then they came towards him, rushing. Towards Ibrahim, after they returned and saw their idols broken and searched for the one who broke them, they assumed that it was him, as explained in His saying: مَنْ فَعَلَ هذا بِآلِهَتِنا ** (Who has done this to our gods?) [the rest of] the Ayah. يَزِفُّونَ (rushing), meaning they hasten (يسرعون). And it was recited in the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول), meaning they are carried upon the rushing (الزّفيف).

(٩٥) قالَ أَتَعْبُدُونَ ما تَنْحِتُونَ ** (95) He said, 'Do you worship what you carve? What you carve from the idols. (٩٦) وَاللهُ خَلَقَكُمْ وَما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (96) while Allah has created you and what you make.' And what you make, for its essence is by His creation, and its carving is by His power (باقتداره). (٩٧) قالُوا ابْنُوا لَهُ بُنْياناً فَأَلْقُوهُ فِي الْجَحِيمِ ** (97) They said, 'Build a structure for him, and cast him into the Blaze.' Into the intense fire. (٩٨) فَأَرادُوا بِهِ كَيْداً ** (98) So they sought to scheme against him, For when he overcame them with the proof (الحجّة), they intended to torture him with that so that their inability (عجزهم) would not become apparent to the common people. ** فَجَعَلْناهُمُ الْأَسْفَلِينَ ** but We made them the lowest. The most abased (الأذلّين) by nullifying their scheme and making it a luminous proof (برهاناً منيراً) of the loftiness of his status, since He made the fire cool and safe for him. His story has already passed in Sūrat al-Anbiyā'.

(٩٩) وَقالَ إِنِّي ذاهِبٌ إِلى رَبِّي سَيَهْدِينِ (99) He said, 'Indeed, I am going to my Lord; He will guide me.' In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He meant بيت المقدس.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in response to one who was confused about the verses, he said: "And I have certainly informed you that the interpretation (تأويله) of many things in the Book of Allah is different from its literal revelation (تنزيله), and it does not resemble the speech of humans. And I shall inform you of a part of it, which will suffice, if Allah wills, such as the saying of Abraham: إِنِّي ذاهِبٌ إِلى رَبِّي سَيَهْدِينِ ** So, his going (ذهابه) to his Lord is his turning toward Him in worship (عبادة), striving (اجتهاداً), and seeking proximity (قربة) to Allah, majestic and mighty is He. Do you not see that its interpretation (تأويله) is different from its literal revelation (تنزيله)?"

(100) ** رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** (100) 'My Lord! Grant me [an heir] from among the righteous.' [Meaning:] some of the righteous ones who will assist me in the call (الدعوة) and obedience (الطاعة), and keep me company in the solitude (الغربة). This means the child (الولد), because the word al-hibah (الهبة) (grant/gift) is predominant concerning him.

footnote (1) And it was said that its meaning is related to the oath (*القسم*) that preceded, which is His saying: **تَاللهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصْنامَكُمْ.**

(١٠١) فَبَشَّرْناهُ بِغُلامٍ حَلِيمٍ ** (101) So We gave him the good news of a gentle son. It was said that Allah did not describe any prophet with forbearance (الحلم) due to the rarity of its existence, other than Abraham (إبراهيم) and his son. (١٠٢) فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ السَّعْيَ ** (102) When he was old enough to work with him, meaning, فَلَمَّا ** (When) he was found and reached the age to strive (يسعى) with him in his works, قالَ يا بُنَيَّ إِنِّي أَرى فِي الْمَنامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحُكَ فَانْظُرْ ما ذا تَرى ** he said, 'My son, I see in a dream that I am sacrificing you. See what you think.' concerning the opinion (الرأي). It was said that He consulted him, even though it was a certainty (حتم), so that He might know what he possessed regarding the trial (بلاء) of Allah that had descended, and thus He would strengthen his resolve if he showed anxiety (جزع), and feel secure concerning him if he submitted (سلّم), and so that he might prepare his soul for it, making it easier, and earn the reward (المثوبة) through compliance before its occurrence. And it was recited (وقرء) ما ذا ترى (what do you see) with a ḍammah on the tāʾ (ت) and a kasrah on the rāʾ (ر). قالَ يا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ ما تُؤْمَرُ ** He said, 'Father, do whatever you are commanded. whatever you are commanded with. And it was mentioned using the form of the present tense (المضارع) due to the repetition of the dream (الرّؤيا). سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ.** If Allah wills, you will find me to be one of the patient.'

(١٠٣) فَلَمَّا أَسْلَما ** (When the two of them had submitted) They both submitted completely to the command of Allah, or the sacrificed one (الذّبيح) submitted himself, and Abraham (submitted) his son. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): They both recited فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَا (when they both finished the submission (التّسليم)). وَتَلَّهُ لِلْجَبِينِ ** (and he had laid him down on his temple) He threw him down upon his side, so his جبين (temple/side of the forehead) fell upon the ground, and it is one of the two sides of the forehead (الجبهة).

(١٠٤) وَنادَيْناهُ أَنْ يا إِبْراهِيمُ. (104) And We called out to him: 'O Ibrāhīm! (١٠٥) قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ الرُّؤْيا ** (105) You have indeed fulfilled the vision.' by the resolve (العزم) and the execution of what was within your capability concerning that. And the answer (جواب) to "when" (لما) is omitted (محذوف), its estimation (تقديره) is: "what happened, happened" (كان ما كان), from what the situation (الحال) speaks of, and what the speech (المقال) cannot encompass, such as their rejoicing (استبشارهما) and their thanking Allah for what He bestowed upon them, namely the removal of the affliction (رفع البلاء) after it had descended, and the granting of success (التوفيق) for that to which no one else was granted success like it, and the manifestation of their merit (فضلهما) through it over all the worlds, along with the attainment of the great reward (الثواب العظيم) and other things besides that. ** إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. Indeed, We thus reward the good-doers.

(١٠٦) إِنَّ هذا لَهُوَ الْبَلاءُ الْمُبِينُ ** (106) Indeed this is the manifest trial. The manifest trial (الابتلاء البيّن) in which the sincere one is distinguished from others, or the manifest difficulty of the ordeal (المحنة البيّنة الصعوبة), for there is nothing more difficult than it. (١٠٧) وَفَدَيْناهُ بِذِبْحٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (107) and We ransomed him with a great sacrifice. With that which was substituted for him, being great in worth (عظيم القدر) or great in body (الجثّة), being fat (سمين). In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how much time elapsed between the glad tidings given to Abraham (peace be upon him) concerning Ishmael and the glad tidings concerning Isaac. He said: "There were five years between the two glad tidings." Allah, the Glorified, said: فَبَشَّرْناهُ بِغُلامٍ حَلِيمٍ (So We gave him the good news of a gentle son), meaning Ishmael, and this was the first glad tidings Allah gave to Abraham (peace be upon him) regarding a child. And when Isaac (peace be upon him) was born to Abraham from Sarah, and Isaac reached three years of age, Ishmael approached Isaac while he was in Abraham's lap, and he pushed him away and sat in his place. Sarah saw this and said: "O Abraham, remove the son of Hagar from my lap, and he sits in his place! No, by Allah, Hagar and her son shall never live near me in this land. Remove them from me." Abraham (peace be upon him) honored Sarah, respected her, and recognized her right, because she was from the progeny of the Prophets and the daughter of his maternal aunt. This was difficult for Abraham (peace be upon him), and he grieved over the separation from Ishmael. When night fell, a visitor came to Abraham from his Lord and showed him the vision concerning the sacrifice of his son Ishmael (peace be upon him) during the season (موسم) of Mecca. Abraham (peace be upon him) awoke saddened by the vision He had seen. When the season of that year arrived, Abraham (peace be upon him) carried Hagar and Ishmael in Dhul-Hijjah from the land of Syria (ارض الشام) and set off with them to Mecca to sacrifice him during the season. He began with the foundations of the Sacred House (البيت الحرام). When He raised its foundations, He went out to Mina as a pilgrim, performed His rites (نسكه) in Mina, then returned to Mecca and circumambulated the House for a week. Then they set off. When they reached the Sa'y (running between Safa and Marwah), Abraham (peace be upon him) said to Ishmael: يا بُنَيَّ إِنِّي أَرى فِي الْمَنامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحُكَ (O my son! I see in a dream that I am sacrificing you) this season, this year. ما ذا تَرى قالَ يا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ ما تُؤْمَرُ (What do you think? He said, 'Father, do whatever you are commanded'). When they finished their Sa'y, Abraham (peace be upon him) set off with him to Mina, and that was the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر). When he reached the Middle Jamrah (الجمرة الوسطى), he laid him down on his left side and took the knife (الشّفرة) to sacrifice him. He was called out to: أَنْ يا إِبْراهِيمُ (O Abraham!) قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ الرُّؤْيا (You have indeed fulfilled the vision) until the end [of the verse]. And Ishmael (peace be upon him) was ransomed with a great ram, which He sacrificed and gave its meat in charity to the poor. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the subject of the sacrifice (صاحب الذبح), and He said: "It is Ishmael (peace be upon him)." And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): the like of it. And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the like of it. In al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked who the sacrificed one (الذبيح) was, and He said: "Ishmael (peace be upon him), because Allah, the Exalted, mentioned his story in His Book, and then said: وَبَشَّرْناهُ بِإِسْحاقَ نَبِيًّا مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (and We gave him the good news of Isaac, a prophet, one of the righteous)." He said: The narrations (الرّوايات) concerning the sacrificed one (الذبيح) have differed. Some of them state that it was Ishmael (peace be upon him), and some state that it was Isaac. There is no way to reject the reports (الاخبار) if their chains of transmission (طرقها) are sound. The sacrificed one was Ishmael, but Isaac, when he was born afterward, wished that he had been the one whom his father was commanded to sacrifice, and that he would endure the command of Allah and submit to Him, just as his brother endured and submitted, so that he might attain his degree of reward (الثواب). Allah knew this from his heart, so Allah named him the sacrificed one (ذبيحاً) among His angels because of his wish for that. He said: I have mentioned the chain of transmission (اسناد ذلك) for this in Kitab al-Nubuwwah (كتاب النبوّة), connected to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). I say: What supports this is that the glad tidings concerning Isaac were coupled with the birth of Jacob, so the command to sacrifice him while he was an adolescent (مراهقاً) is not appropriate. In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): They mentioned that when it was the Day of Tarwiyah (يوم التروية), Gabriel said to Abraham (peace be upon him): "Take provision (تروّ) of water," so it was named al-Tarwiyah. Then He came to Mina and made him spend the night there. Then He went with him to Arafat and pitched his tent (خِباه) in Namirah, short of Arafah. He built a mosque there with white stones, and the trace of Abraham's mosque (peace be upon him) was known until it was incorporated into this mosque in Namirah, where the Imam prays on the Day of Arafah. He prayed the Noon (الظهر) and Afternoon (العصر) prayers there. Then He proceeded with him to Arafat and said: "This is Arafat, so recognize (اعرف) your rites (مناسكك) here and confess (اعترف) your sin," so it was named Arafat. Then He proceeded to Muzdalifah, and it was named Muzdalifah because He drew near (ازدلف) to it. Then He stood upon al-Mash'ar al-Haram (المشعر الحرام). Allah commanded Him to sacrifice His son, in whom He had seen His noble traits (شمائله) and characteristics (خلائقه), and to whom He was attached. When morning came, He proceeded from al-Mash'ar to Mina. He said to his mother [Hagar]: "You visit the House, and detain the boy." He said: "O my son, bring the donkey and the knife (السكين) so that I may offer the sacrifice (القربان)." The narrator was asked what He meant by the donkey and the knife. He said: "He intended to sacrifice him, then carry him, prepare him, and bury him." He said: The boy brought the donkey and the knife. He said: "O father, where is the sacrifice?" He said: "Your Lord knows where it is. O my son, by Allah, you are it! Indeed, Allah has commanded me to sacrifice you. فَانْظُرْ ما ذا تَرى قالَ يا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ ما تُؤْمَرُ سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ (So consider what you think.' He said, 'Father, do whatever you are commanded. If Allah wills, you will find me among the patient')." He said: When He resolved upon the sacrifice, He said: "O father, cover my face and tie my bonds (وثاقي)." He said: "O my son, bonds (الوثاق) along with sacrifice? By Allah, I will not combine them upon you today." Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) said: He spread the donkey's saddle-cloth (قرطان) for him, then laid him down upon it, took the knife (المدية), and placed it on his throat. He said: Then an old man approached and said: "What do you want with this boy?" He said: "I want to sacrifice him." He said: "Subhan Allah (سبحان الله)! A boy who has not disobeyed Allah for the blink of an eye—you sacrifice him?" He said: "Yes, Allah has commanded me to sacrifice him." He said: "Rather, your Lord forbids you from sacrificing him, and it was only Satan who commanded you this in your dream." He said: "Woe upon you! The speech I heard is what brought me to what you see. No, by Allah, I will not speak to you." Then He resolved upon the sacrifice. The old man said: "O Abraham, you are an Imam (إمام) whom people follow. If you sacrifice your son, people will sacrifice their sons. Be patient (مهلاً)." But He refused to speak to him. Then He (peace be upon him) said: He laid him down near the Middle Jamrah, then took the knife and placed it on his throat, then raised His head to the sky, then lunged at him with the knife. Gabriel (peace be upon him) turned it away from his throat. Abraham looked and saw that it was turned over. Abraham (peace be upon him) turned it back to its edge, and Gabriel (peace be upon him) turned it back to its blunt side (قفاها). He did this repeatedly. Then a call came from the left side (ميسرة) of the Mosque of al-Khayf (1): يا إِبْراهِيمُ قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ الرُّؤْيا (O Abraham! You have indeed fulfilled the vision). And the boy was pulled out from beneath Him, and Gabriel took the ram from the peak (قُلَّة) of Thabir (بثير) and placed it beneath Him. The wicked old man (الشيخ الخبيث) went out until He caught up with the old woman [Sarah] when she looked toward the House, and the House was in the middle of the valley. He said: "What about an old man I saw in Mina?" He described Abraham (peace be upon him). She said: "That is my husband (بعلي)." He said: "And what about a young servant (وصيف) I saw with him?" He described him. She said: "That is my son." He said: "I saw him lay him down and take the knife to sacrifice him." She said: "Never! You did not see him. Abraham (peace be upon him) is the most merciful of people. How could you see him sacrificing his son?" He said: "By the Lord of the heaven and the earth, and the Lord of this structure (البنية), I indeed saw him lay him down and take the knife to sacrifice him." She asked: "Why?" He said: "He claimed that his Lord commanded him to sacrifice him." She said: "Then it is his right to obey his Lord." He said: When she finished her rites (مناسكها), she became worried that something might have befallen her son. It is as if I see her rushing in the valley, placing her hand on her head, and saying: "My Lord, do not hold me accountable for what I did to the mother of Ishmael (امّ إسماعيل)." He said: When Sarah came and was informed of the news, she went to look at her son, and behold, the trace of the knife was scratches (خدوشاً) on his throat. She was terrified and fell ill, and that was the beginning of the illness in which she perished. He (peace be upon him) said: He intended to sacrifice him in the place where the mother of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was conceived, near the Middle Jamrah. Their resting place (مضربهم) continued to be inherited generation after generation until the last one who departed from it was Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them) due to something that occurred between Banu Hashim and Banu Umayyah, so He departed and pitched his tent in al-Arin. And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that which is close to it, with additions and omissions. And al-Qummi added: The ram descended upon the mountain that is to the right of the Mosque of Mina. It descended from the heaven, and it ate in black, walked in black, and was horned (اقرن). It was asked: "What was its color?" He said: "It was speckled (أملح), dust-colored (أغبر)." In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), He said: When Allah, the Exalted, commanded Abraham (peace be upon him) to sacrifice the ram that was sent down to him instead of his son Ishmael (peace be upon him), Abraham (peace be upon him) wished that He had sacrificed his son Ishmael (peace be upon him) with His own hand, and that He had not been commanded to sacrifice the ram in his place, so that His heart might experience what the heart of a father experiences when He sacrifices his dearest child with His own hand, thereby deserving the highest degrees of those rewarded for calamities (المصائب). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Him: "O Abraham, who is the most beloved of My creation to you?" He said: "O Lord, You have not created any creature more beloved to me than Your Beloved, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Him: "O Abraham, is He more beloved to you, or your own soul?" He said: "Rather, He is more beloved to me than my own soul." He said: "And is His progeny more beloved to you, or your progeny?" He said: "Rather, His progeny." He said: "Is the unjust slaughter of His progeny by the hands of His enemies more painful to your heart, or the sacrifice of your son by your hand in obedience to Me?" He said: "O Lord, rather, their slaughter by the hands of His enemies is more painful to my heart." He said: "O Abraham, a faction that claims to be from the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله will unjustly and aggressively kill al-Husayn (peace be upon him), His son, after Him, just as the ram is slaughtered, and thereby they will incur My wrath. Abraham (peace be upon him) grieved over this, His heart ached, and He began to weep. So Allah, the Exalted, revealed to Him: "O Abraham, I have ransomed your grief over your son Ishmael (peace be upon him), had you sacrificed him with your hand, with your grief over al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and his killing, and I have made incumbent upon you the highest degrees of those rewarded for calamities." And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَفَدَيْناهُ بِذِبْحٍ عَظِيمٍ (and We ransomed him with a great sacrifice). And there is no power and no strength except with Allah, the Most High, the Almighty. And He was asked about the meaning of the saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "I am the son of the two sacrificed ones (الذبيحين)." He said: "He means Ishmael son of Abraham the Intimate Friend (الخليل) (peace be upon them) and Abdullah son of Abdul Muttalib. As for Ishmael, He is the gentle son (الغلام الحليم) with whom Allah, the Exalted, gave Abraham (peace be upon him) glad tidings: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ السَّعْيَ (When he was old enough to work with him), meaning when He performed actions like His actions. قالَ يا بُنَيَّ إِنِّي أَرى فِي الْمَنامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحُكَ فَانْظُرْ ما ذا تَرى قالَ يا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ ما تُؤْمَرُ سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ (He said, 'O my son! I see in a dream that I am sacrificing you. So consider what you think.' He said, 'Father, do whatever you are commanded. If Allah wills, you will find me among the patient'). When He resolved upon sacrificing him, Allah ransomed him with a great sacrifice: a speckled ram (كبش أملح) that ate in black, drank in black, looked in black, walked in black, and urinated and defecated in black. Before that, it had grazed in the gardens of Paradise (رياض الجنّة) for forty years, and it did not come out of the womb of a female (رحم أنثى). Rather, Allah, the Exalted, said to it: 'Be,' and it was, so that Ishmael (peace be upon him) might be ransomed by it. Thus, everything sacrificed in Mina is a ransom for Ishmael until the Day of Resurrection. This is one of the two sacrificed ones (الذبيحين)." Then He mentioned the story of the other sacrificed one. Then He said: "The reason (العلّة) for which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, averted the sacrifice from Ishmael (peace be upon him) is the same reason for which Allah averted the sacrifice from Abdullah, which is the presence of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imams (peace be upon them) in their loins (صلبهما). By the blessing of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imams (peace be upon them), Allah averted the sacrifice from both of them (peace be upon them). Thus, the practice (السنة) of killing their children was not established among people. Were it not for this, it would have been obligatory upon people every Adha (اضحى) to seek nearness to Allah, glorified be His remembrance, by killing their children. And every sacrifice (اضحية) by which people seek nearness [to Allah] is a ransom for Ishmael until the Day of Resurrection." In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Had Allah created a morsel (مضغة) that was better than mutton (الضّان), He would have ransomed Ishmael (peace be upon him) with it."

(1) Al-Khayf (الخيف) is that which slopes down from the thickness of the mountain and rises above the watercourse (مسيل الماء). From this, the Mosque of al-Khayf in Mina was named.

(١٠٨) وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرِينَ. (108) And We preserved his good name among the later generations. (١٠٩) سَلامٌ عَلى إِبْراهِيمَ (ع) Peace be to Abraham! (peace be upon him) Its explanation preceded in the story of Noah (peace be upon him). (١١٠) كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (110) Thus do We reward the good-doers. (١١١) إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (111) Indeed, he was one of Our faithful servants. (١١٢) وَبَشَّرْناهُ بِإِسْحاقَ نَبِيًّا مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ. (112) And We gave him the good news of Isaac, a prophet, one of the righteous. (١١٣) وَبارَكْنا عَلَيْهِ upon Abraham (peace be upon him) وَعَلى إِسْحاقَ We poured upon them both the blessings (بركات) of the religion (الدّين) and the world (الدّنيا). وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِما مُحْسِنٌ وَظالِمٌ لِنَفْسِهِ through disbelief (بالكفر) and acts of disobedience (والمعاصي), clearly (مُبِينٌ), whose wrongdoing (ظلمه) is manifest (ظاهر). And in that is an alert (تنبيه) that lineage (النسب) has no effect (أثر) on guidance (الهدى) or misguidance (الضلال), and that the wrongdoing (الظلم) among their progeny (أعقابهما) does not revert to them with deficiency (نقيصة) or fault (عيب). (113) We blessed him and Isaac. Yet among their descendants are those who are good-doers and those who clearly wrong themselves.

(١١٤) ** وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلى مُوسى وَهارُونَ ** (114) And We certainly favoured Moses and Aaron, We bestowed favor upon them both with prophethood (النبوّة) and other religious (الدينيّة) and worldly (الدنيويّة) benefits. (١١٥) ** وَنَجَّيْناهُما وَقَوْمَهُما مِنَ الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (115) and delivered them and their people from the great distress, From the dominance of Pharaoh (الفرعون) or drowning (الغرق). (١١٦) ** وَنَصَرْناهُمْ فَكانُوا هُمُ الْغالِبِينَ ** (116) and helped them, so they were the victors. Over Pharaoh (فرعون) and his people. (١١٧) ** وَآتَيْناهُمَا الْكِتابَ الْمُسْتَبِينَ ** (117) And We gave them the illuminating Book, The eloquent (البليغ) in its clarification, which is the Torah (التوراة). (١١٨) ** وَهَدَيْناهُمَا الصِّراطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ ** (118) and guided them to the straight path. The path leading to truth (الحقّ) and correctness (الصواب). (١١٩) ** وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِما فِي الْآخِرِينَ. (119) And We left for them among the later generations: (١٢٠) ** سَلامٌ عَلى مُوسى وَهارُونَ. (120) ‘Peace be to Moses and Aaron!’ (١٢١) ** إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (121) Thus do We reward the good-doers. (١٢٢) ** إِنَّهُما مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (122) Indeed, they were among Our faithful servants. The like of this has preceded. (١٢٣) ** وَإِنَّ إِلْياسَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (123) Indeed, Ilyas was one of the apostles. (١٢٤) ** إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ أَلا تَتَّقُونَ. (124) When he said to his people, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (١٢٥) ** أَتَدْعُونَ بَعْلاً ** (125) Do you invoke Ba‘l, Do you worship it and seek goodness from it? Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: They had an idol (صنم) they called بَعْلاً . He said: And the Lord (الربّ) was named Ba‘l (بعلاً). وَتَذَرُونَ أَحْسَنَ الْخالِقِينَ ** And you abandon His worship. (١٢٦) ** اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ وَرَبَّ آبائِكُمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (126) and abandon the best of creators—Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?’ And it was recited (وقرئ) with the nasb (النّصب) case (accusative). (١٢٧) ** فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ ** (127) But they denied him, so they will indeed be arraigned, Meaning, in the punishment (العذاب). (١٢٨) ** إِلَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (128) except the sincere servants of Allah. This is an exception (مستثنى) from the waw (in كَذَّبُوهُ), not from الْمُحْضَرُونَ (those arraigned), due to the corruption of the meaning. (١٢٩) ** وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرِينَ. (129) And We left for him among the later generations: (١٣٠) ** سَلامٌ عَلى إِلْ ياسِينَ. (130) ‘Peace be to Ilyasin!’ Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and said: ** وَتَرَكْنا عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرِينَ سَلامٌ عَلى إِلْ ياسِينَ. He said: يس ** is Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد), the Imams (peace be upon them). In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers, from Ali (peace be upon them) regarding this Ayah, he said: Yasin (يس) is Muhammad, and we are Il Yasin (إِلْ ياسِينَ.). In al-Jawami', narrated from Ibn Abbas: Al Yasin (آلْ ياسِينَ) is the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), and Yasin (يس ) is one of His names. News regarding the naming of the Prophet (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) as Yasin has already passed in Surat al-Ahzab at His saying, the Almighty: وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً (and greet [him] with a [worthy] greeting) and at the beginning of Surah Yasin. This reading (القراءة) is supported by the fact that they (إِلْ and ياسِينَ) are separated in the mushaf (مصحف) of their Imam. And إِلْ ياسِينَ was read. It was said that it is a dialect (لغة) of Ilyas (الياس), like Sina (سينا) and Sinin (سنين). And it was said that it is a plural (جمع) for him, intended to mean him and his followers. However, concerning this, if it were so, it would have been definite (معرّفاً). And it was said that يس ** is the name of the father of Ilyas, according to the reading of Al Yasin (آل ياسين), to correspond with what follows it and the structure (نظم) of the rest of the stories, just as in the reading of Ilyasin (الياسين). In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, Allah named the Prophet with this name where He said: يس وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (Yasin. By the wise Quran, You are indeed one of the apostles), knowing that they would drop "Peace be upon the Family of Muhammad" (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) just as they dropped other things. And in this is an indication of the reading of Al Yasin (آل يس) and that the intended meaning is the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

(١٣١) إِنَّا كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (131) Indeed, thus do We reward the good-doers. (١٣٢) إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (132) Indeed, he was one of Our faithful servants. (١٣٣) وَإِنَّ لُوطاً لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (133) And indeed Lot was one of the apostles. (١٣٤) إِذْ نَجَّيْناهُ وَأَهْلَهُ أَجْمَعِينَ. (134) When We delivered him and his family, all of them, (١٣٥) إِلَّا عَجُوزاً فِي الْغابِرِينَ. (135) except an old woman who was among those who remained behind. (١٣٦) ثُمَّ دَمَّرْنَا الْآخَرِينَ ** (136) Then We destroyed the others. And its exegesis has already passed. (١٣٧) وَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (137) And indeed you pass by them It was said: meaning, by their dwellings during your trade journeys to al-Shām (Syria), for Sadūm is on its path مُصْبِحِينَ ** entering into the morning. (١٣٨) وَبِاللَّيْلِ أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ ** (138) and by night. Will you not then apply reason? Is there not among you reason (عقل) by which you may take heed?

In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "You pass by them in the Quran; when you read the Quran, you read what Allah has recounted to you of their story."

(١٣٩) وَإِنَّ يُونُسَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (139) Indeed, Jonah was one of the apostles. (١٤٠) إِذْ أَبَقَ إِلَى الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ ** (140) when he fled to the laden ship. هرب (fled). The origin of al-ibāq (الإباق) is the flight of a slave from his master (السيّد), but since his flight from his people was without the permission of his Lord, its application to him is appropriate. المملوّ (filled/loaded). (١٤١) فَساهَمَ فَكانَ مِنَ الْمُدْحَضِينَ ** (141) Then he cast lots, and was among the vanquished. فقارع اهله (He cast lots with its people). So he became one of those overcome by the lot (القرعة), and its origin (أصله) is the one slipped away (المزلق) from the position of victory (مقام الظفر).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: When he embarked with the people, the ship stopped in the deep sea (اللّجة). They cast lots, and the lot fell upon Jonah three times. He (peace be upon him) said: So Jonah went to the bow of the ship, and behold, the whale was opening its mouth, so he threw himself in.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): No people ever cast lots and entrusted their affair to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, except that the lot of the rightful person (المحقّ) came out. He (peace be upon him) said: What judgment is more just than the lot (القرعة) when they entrust the affair to Allah? Does not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: فَساهَمَ فَكانَ مِنَ الْمُدْحَضِينَ. (Then he cast lots, and was among the vanquished.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): That which is close to it (in meaning).

(142) ** فَالْتَقَمَهُ الْحُوتُ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٌ ** (142) Then the fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy. [He was] entering into blame (الْمَلَامَة), or bringing forth that for which he is blamed, or blaming himself (مُلِيمٌ نَفْسَهُ).

Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), concerning the story of Yunus and his people—the beginning of which was mentioned previously in its surah—said: So Yunus became angry and went off on his way, being angry with Allah, as Allah recounted, until he reached the seashore. There, he found a ship that had been loaded and they intended to push it off. Yunus asked them to carry him, and they carried him. When they reached the middle of the sea, Allah sent a great fish (حُوتًا عَظِيمًا) which detained the ship for them. Yunus looked at it and was terrified of it, so he went to the stern of the ship. The fish turned towards him and opened its mouth. The people of the ship came out and said: "There is a disobedient one (عَاصٍ) among us." So they cast lots (تَسَاهَمُوا), and Yunus came out [as the one chosen]. And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَساهَمَ فَكانَ مِنَ الْمُدْحَضِينَ ** (So he cast lots, and was among the vanquished.) So they brought him out and cast him into the sea, and [the fish] swallowed him (فَالْتَقَمَهُ) and passed with him through the water.

(١٤٣) فَلَوْ لا أَنَّهُ كانَ مِنَ الْمُسَبِّحِينَ ** (143) Had it not been that he was one of the glorifiers, Those who remember Allah frequently through glorification (التسبيح). (١٤٤) لَلَبِثَ فِي بَطْنِهِ إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ. (144) he would have remained in its belly till the day they will be resurrected. (١٤٥) فَنَبَذْناهُ بِالْعَراءِ ** (145) Then We cast him on the bare ground, In the empty place devoid of anything that might cover him, such as trees or plants, while he was sick from what had afflicted him. (١٤٦) وَأَنْبَتْنا عَلَيْهِ شَجَرَةً مِنْ يَقْطِينٍ ** (146) and We caused a gourd plant to grow over him. From a plant that spreads out upon the surface of the earth and does not stand upon a stalk. Al-Qummi said: It is the squash (الدّبا). (١٤٧) وَأَرْسَلْناهُ إِلى مِائَةِ أَلْفٍ أَوْ يَزِيدُونَ. (147) And We sent him to a hundred thousand or more. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited وَيَزِيدُونَ (and more) with the waw (الواو). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): they exceed by thirty thousand. (١٤٨) فَآمَنُوا فَمَتَّعْناهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ ** (148) So they believed, and We provided them with comfort for a while. Until their decreed term (أجلهم المقضّى). Al-Qummi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The whale traveled with him through the regions of the earth and the seas and passed by Qarun, until [Yunus] said: Then he cried out in the darknesses, 'There is no god except You! Glory be to You! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers!' as was previously mentioned in Surat al-Qasas. He said: So He responded to him, and He commanded the whale to cast him out, so it cast him out onto the seashore, and his skin and flesh had wasted away. And Allah caused a gourd plant to grow over him, which is the squash (الدّبا). It shaded him from the sun, so he rested. Then Allah commanded the plant, and it withdrew from him, and the sun fell upon him, so he became distressed. Then Allah revealed to him: "O Yunus, why did you not show mercy to a hundred thousand or more, yet you are distressed by the heat of the sun (الم ساعة)?" He said: "O Lord, Your pardon, Your pardon." So Allah restored his body to him, and he returned to his people, and they believed in Him. And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Yunus remained in the belly of the whale for three days. Then he cried out in the darknesses—the darkness of the whale's belly, the darkness of the night, and the darkness of the sea—'There is no god except You! Glory be to You! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers!' So his Lord responded to him, and the whale brought him out to the shore, then it cast him forth and threw him onto the shore. And Allah caused a gourd plant to grow over him, which is the pumpkin (القرع). He would suckle from it and seek shade under it and its leaves. His hair had fallen out and his skin had become thin. Yunus would glorify Allah and remember Allah by night and day. When he became strong and recovered, Allah sent a worm that ate the bottom of the gourd, so the gourd withered and then dried up. This distressed Yunus, and he remained sorrowful. Then Allah revealed to him: "Why are you sorrowful, O Yunus?" He said: "O Lord, this plant that benefited me, You set a worm upon it, and it dried up." He said: "O Yunus, you grieved for a plant that you neither planted nor watered nor cared for, when it dried up after you had become independent of it. Yet you did not grieve for the people of Nineveh, more than a hundred thousand, upon whom the punishment was about to descend. Indeed, the people of Nineveh have believed and become righteous (اتّقوا). So return to them." So Yunus set out toward his people. When he drew near to Nineveh, he was ashamed to enter. He said to a shepherd whom he met: "Go to the people of Nineveh and tell them that this Yunus has come." The shepherd said: "Are you lying? Have you no shame? Yunus drowned in the sea and is gone." Yunus said to him: "O Allah, this sheep bears witness for You that I am Yunus." And the sheep spoke to him, confirming that he was Yunus. When the shepherd came to his people and informed them, they seized him and intended to strike him. He said: "I have proof (بيّنة) for what I say." They asked: "Who testifies for you?" He said: "This sheep testifies." So it testified that he was truthful and that Allah had returned Yunus to them. They went out seeking him and found him. They brought him, and they believed, and their faith was excellent. So Allah provided them with comfort until a term (إلى حين), which is death, and He protected them from that punishment.

(١٤٩) فَاسْتَفْتِهِمْ أَلِرَبِّكَ الْبَناتُ وَلَهُمُ الْبَنُونَ ** (149) So ask them: ‘Are the daughters for your Lord, and the sons for them?’ Al-Qummi said: Quraysh said that the angels are the daughters of Allah, so Allah refuted them. (١٥٠) أَمْ خَلَقْنَا الْمَلائِكَةَ إِناثاً وَهُمْ شاهِدُونَ ** (150) Did We create the angels as females while they were present? Since it is not possible to know such a thing except through witnessing (بِالْمُشَاهَدَة). (١٥١) أَلا إِنَّهُمْ مِنْ إِفْكِهِمْ لَيَقُولُونَ (151) Look! It is out of their falsehood that they say: (١٥٢) وَلَدَ اللهُ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ** (152) ‘Allah has begotten!’ And they are indeed liars, regarding that which they profess as religion (يَتَدَيَّنُونَ بِهِ). (١٥٣) أَصْطَفَى الْبَناتِ عَلَى الْبَنِينَ ** (153) Has He chosen daughters over sons? This is an interrogative expressing denial (إِنْكَار) and deeming it improbable (اسْتِبْعَاد). It was recited with the kasrah on the hamzah (أَصْطَفَى) by omitting the hamzah (of the interrogative) because the أَمْ (or) that follows indicates it, or by implying the saying (بِإِضْمَارِ الْقَوْل), meaning: they are liars in their saying, "He has chosen." (١٥٤) ما لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ ** (154) What is the matter with you? How do you judge? with that which reason (عَقْل) does not approve. (١٥٥) أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (155) Will you not then take admonition? that He is exalted (مُنَزَّه) above that. (١٥٦) أَمْ لَكُمْ سُلْطانٌ مُبِينٌ ** (156) Do you have a manifest authority? a clear proof (حُجَّةٌ وَاضِحَة) that has descended upon you from the heaven, [stating] that the angels are His daughters. (١٥٧) فَأْتُوا بِكِتابِكُمْ ** (157) Then bring your scripture, which was sent down upon you, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful in your claim (دَعْوَاكُمْ). (١٥٨) وَجَعَلُوا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْجِنَّةِ نَسَباً. (158) And they have set up a kinship between Him and the jinn. Al-Qummi said: It means that they said the jinn (الْجِنّ) are the daughters of Allah. And it was said: It means the angels, who were named by this [term] due to their concealment (لِاسْتِتَارِهِمْ). And it was said: They claimed that Allah married the jinn (صَاهَرَ الْجِنّ) and the angels emerged [from that union]. And it was said: They claimed that Allah and Satan (الشَّيْطَان) are brothers—highly exalted is Allah above what the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمُونَ) say, with great exaltation (عُلُوّاً كَبِيراً). وَلَقَدْ عَلِمَتِ الْجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمْ ** (And certainly the jinn know that they) that the polytheists (الْمُشْرِكِينَ) لَمُحْضَرُونَ. (will surely be arraigned.) Al-Qummi said: It means إِنَّهُمْ ** (that they) [will be] in the Fire (فِي النَّار).

(159) سُبْحانَ اللهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ ** (159) Clear is Allah of what they attribute [to Him]! Regarding offspring and lineage. (160) إِلَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ. (160) —except the sincere servants of Allah. (161) فَإِنَّكُمْ وَما تَعْبُدُونَ ** (161) For indeed, you and what you worship A return to addressing them. (162) ما أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (162) cannot tempt [anyone] against Him, against Allah بِفاتِنِينَ ** (tempters), meaning: corrupting the people through misguidance (الإغواء). (163) إِلَّا مَنْ هُوَ صالِ الْجَحِيمِ ** (163) except him who is bound for the Hellfire. Except for him concerning whom it was predetermined in His knowledge that he is one of the people of the Fire, who will inevitably enter it. (164) وَما مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ مَقامٌ مَعْلُومٌ.** (164) There is none among us but has a known station.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: It was revealed concerning the Imams and the Successors (الأوصياء) from the Family of Muhammad (may the prayers of Allah be upon them).

And it was said that this is a narration of the angels' confession of servitude (العبودية) in refutation of those who worship them. The meaning is: وَما مِنَّا ** (There is none among us) —meaning, no one— إِلَّا لَهُ مَقامٌ مَعْلُومٌ ** (but has a known station) in knowledge (المعرفة), worship (العبادة), and adherence to the command of Allah in the administration (تدبير) of the universe. It was said that it is also probable that the saying ** سُبْحانَ اللهِ ** (Clear is Allah) is a narration of their saying (the angels' saying).

(١٦٥) وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الصَّافُّونَ ** (165) And indeed, it is we who are the ranged ones in performing obedience (الطّاعة) and in the stations of service (الخدمة). (١٦٦) وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْمُسَبِّحُونَ ** (166) and indeed, it is we who are the glorifiers. those who declare Allah free from what is unfitting for Him. And perhaps the first [verse] is a reference to their ranks in obedience (الطاعة), and this [verse] is concerning knowledge (المعرفة). In Nahj al-Balagha, in the description of the angels: "Ranged (صافّون), they do not separate, and glorifiers (مسبّحون), they do not tire." And al-Qummi [narrated]: Gabriel said: "O Muhammad, إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الصَّافُّونَ وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْمُسَبِّحُونَ."

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "We were lights, standing in rows (صفوفاً) around the Throne, glorifying [Allah], and the inhabitants of the heaven would glorify [Him] by our glorification, until we descended to the earth. Then we glorified [Him], and the inhabitants of the earth glorified [Him] by our glorification. وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الصَّافُّونَ وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْمُسَبِّحُونَ. The hadith."

(١٦٧) وَإِنْ كانُوا لَيَقُولُونَ ** (167) And they used to say: meaning, the polytheists of Quraysh (مشركوا قريش). (١٦٨) لَوْ أَنَّ عِنْدَنا ذِكْراً مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (168) 'If only we had a reminder from the ancients, (meaning) a book (كتاباً) from the books that were revealed to them. (١٦٩) لَكُنَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (169) we would surely have been Allah's exclusive servants.' We would have made worship exclusive to Him (أخلصنا العبادة له) and would not have opposed (the truth) like them. (١٧٠) فَكَفَرُوا بِهِ ** (170) But they disbelieved in it. when the Reminder (الذكر) came to them, which is the noblest of all reminders and the dominant one over them. In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the disbelievers of Quraysh (كفّار قريش) who used to say: لَوْ أَنَّ عِنْدَنا ذِكْراً مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (If only we had a reminder from the ancients). "May Allah fight the Jews and the Christians! How they denied their prophets! By Allah, if we had عِنْدَنا ذِكْراً مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ لَكُنَّا عِبادَ اللهِ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (a reminder from the ancients, we would surely have been Allah's exclusive servants)." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), says: فَكَفَرُوا بِهِ ** (But they disbelieved in it) when Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny) brought it to them. فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (Soon they will know) the consequence (عاقبة) of their disbelief.

(١٧١) وَلَقَدْ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنا لِعِبادِنَا الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (171) Certainly Our Word has already gone forth for Our servants, the apostles: meaning Our promise to them of victory (النصر) and dominance (الغلبة), which is His saying: (١٧٢) إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ الْمَنْصُورُونَ. (172) they will indeed be the helped ones, (١٧٣) وَإِنَّ جُنْدَنا لَهُمُ الْغالِبُونَ. (173) and Our hosts will indeed be the victors. (١٧٤) فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** (174) So turn away from them so turn away from them حَتَّى حِينٍ ** (until a time), which is the appointed time (الموعد) for your victory over them. It was said that it is the Day of Badr (يوم بدر), and it was said that it is the Day of the Conquest (يوم الفتح). (١٧٥) وَأَبْصِرْهُمْ ** (175) and watch them, regarding what will befall them at that time. The intent of the command (الأمر) is to indicate that this will occur soon, as if it were right before him فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ ** (for soon they will see) what We have decreed for you of support (التأييد), victory (النصرة), and reward (الثواب) in the Hereafter. And the word سوف (soon) is for warning (الوعيد), not for postponement (التّبعيد).

(١٧٦) ** أَفَبِعَذابِنا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ ** (176) Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? It is narrated that when فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ ** (then soon they will see) was revealed, they said, "When is this?" So, [this Ayah] was revealed. (١٧٧) ** فَإِذا نَزَلَ بِساحَتِهِمْ ** (177) So when it descends upon their courtyard, So when the punishment descends upon their courtyard (فنائهم), He likened it to an army that attacked them and settled down in their courtyard suddenly. فَساءَ صَباحُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ ** then evil will be the morning of those who have been warned. their morning. It was said that the morning (الصباح) is borrowed metaphorically from the morning attack of the army that spent the night [nearby], referring to the time of the punishment's descent. And because attacks and raids were frequent among them in the morning, they named the raid 'morning' (صباحاً), even if it occurred at another time.

(١٧٨) ** وَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّى حِينٍ. (178) So turn away from them until a time. (١٧٩) ** وَأَبْصِرْ فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ ** (179) And watch, for soon they will watch. (This is) an emphasis upon an emphasis, and an absolute statement after a restricted one, to indicate that he (the Prophet) will watch and they will watch that which the mention cannot encompass, comprising various kinds of joy (المسرّة) and types of distress (المساءة). Or, the first (watching) is for the punishment of the world (الدنيا) and the second is for the punishment of the Hereafter (الآخرة). And Al-Qummi (said regarding the verse): فَإِذا نَزَلَ بِساحَتِهِمْ ** (When it descends upon their courtyard), meaning the punishment (العذاب) when it descends upon Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة) and their followers (أشياعهم) at the end of time (في آخر الزمان). فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ ** (Soon they will watch). He said: They will watch when sight (البصر) will not benefit them. He said: So this (verse) concerns the people of doubts (الشبهات) and misguidance (الضلالات) among the people of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة).

(180) سُبْحانَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (180) Clear is your Lord, the Lord of Might, of what they allege [concerning Him]. [Clear] of what the polytheists said.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Verily, Allah—exalted is His mention (علا ذكره)—was, and there was nothing other than Him. And He was Mighty (عزيزاً), and there was no might (عزّ) that existed before His might (عزّه). And that is His saying, glory be to Him (سُبْحانَهُ): سُبْحانَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ. (Clear is your Lord, the Lord of Might).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] that is close to it.

(١٨١) وَسَلامٌ عَلَى الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (181) And peace be upon the apostles! This is a generalization of the salutation (التسليم) to all the Messengers (الرّسل) after specifying some of them. (١٨٢) وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (182) All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. [This praise is] for what He bestowed upon them and upon those who followed them, of blessings (النّعم) and a good outcome (حُسن العاقبة). And in this is a teaching for the believers on how they should praise Him and send salutations upon His Messengers (رسله).

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Whoever desires to be measured with the fullest measure (المكيال الأوفى) should say, when he intends to rise from his gathering (مجلسه): سُبْحانَ رَبِّكَ ** (The three Ayahs).

In al-Faqih and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to it.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah As-Saffat (سورة الصافّات) every Friday will remain protected from every affliction (آفة), and every calamity (بليّة) will be repelled from him in the life of this world. He will be provided for in this world with the most abundant provision (الرزق), and Allah will not afflict him in his wealth, his children, or his body with any evil from any accursed devil (شيطان رجيم) nor from any obstinate tyrant (جبّار عنيد). And if he dies on that day or night, Allah will resurrect him as a martyr (شهيداً) and admit him to Paradise (الجنّة) with the martyrs in a rank (درجة) of Paradise.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): It (meaning the Ayahs or the Surah) is never recited near one afflicted by death (مكروب من موت) except that Allah, the Almighty, hastens his comfort (راحته), if He wills.
